title: hieracium murorum subsp. tanyptortum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. tanyptortum, also known as wall hawkweed, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family and is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, wall hawkweed has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The plant's medicinal properties also provide health benefits including the reduction of fever, relief from pain, and promotion of digestive health. Wall hawkweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for various methods of consumption and application in traditional medicine.
